comment_text.sn16.6:2.1: A list of such qualities is given at MN 15:3.1.
comment_text.sn16.6:2.2: Bhaṇḍa and Abhiñjika are mentioned only here.

root_text.sn16.6:1.2: Atha kho āyasmā mahākassapo yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ mahākassapaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.6:1.5: Ahaṁ vā, kassapa, bhikkhū ovadeyyaṁ tvaṁ vā;
root_text.sn16.6:1.6: ahaṁ vā bhikkhūnaṁ dhammiṁ kathaṁ kareyyaṁ tvaṁ vā”ti.
root_text.sn16.6:2.1: “Dubbacā kho, bhante, etarahi bhikkhū, dovacassakaraṇehi dhammehi samannāgatā, akkhamā, appadakkhiṇaggāhino anusāsaniṁ.
root_text.sn16.6:2.2: Idhāhaṁ, bhante, addasaṁ bhaṇḍañca nāma bhikkhuṁ ānandassa saddhivihāriṁ abhijikañca nāma bhikkhuṁ anuruddhassa saddhivihāriṁ aññamaññaṁ sutena accāvadante:
root_text.sn16.6:2.3: ‘ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’”ti.
root_text.sn16.6:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn16.6:3.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena bhaṇḍañca bhikkhuṁ ānandassa saddhivihāriṁ abhijikañca bhikkhuṁ anuruddhassa saddhivihāriṁ āmantehi:
root_text.sn16.6:3.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yena te bhikkhū tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā te bhikkhū etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.6:4.1: “Evamāvuso”ti kho te bhikkhū tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkamiṁsu; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁsu. Ekamantaṁ nisinne kho te bhikkhū bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn16.6:4.2: “saccaṁ kira tumhe, bhikkhave, aññamaññaṁ sutena accāvadatha:
root_text.sn16.6:4.3: ‘ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn16.6:4.5: “Kiṁ nu kho me tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānātha:
root_text.sn16.6:4.7: ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’”ti?
root_text.sn16.6:4.9: “No ce kira me tumhe, bhikkhave, evaṁ dhammaṁ desitaṁ ājānātha, atha kiñcarahi tumhe, moghapurisā, kiṁ jānantā kiṁ passantā evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitā samānā aññamaññaṁ sutena accāvadatha:
root_text.sn16.6:4.10: ‘ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’”ti.
root_text.sn16.6:5.1: Atha kho te bhikkhū bhagavato pādesu sirasā nipatitvā bhagavantaṁ etadavocuṁ:
root_text.sn16.6:5.2: “accayo no, bhante, accagamā, yathābāle yathāmūḷhe yathāakusale, ye mayaṁ evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitā samānā aññamaññaṁ sutena accāvadimha:
root_text.sn16.6:5.3: ‘ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’ti.
root_text.sn16.6:5.4: Tesaṁ no, bhante, bhagavā accayaṁ accayato paṭiggaṇhātu āyatiṁ saṁvarāyā”ti.
root_text.sn16.6:6.1: “Taggha tumhe, bhikkhave, accayo accagamā yathābāle yathāmūḷhe yathāakusale, ye tumhe evaṁ svākkhāte dhammavinaye pabbajitā samānā aññamaññaṁ sutena accāvadittha:
root_text.sn16.6:6.2: ‘ehi, bhikkhu, ko bahutaraṁ bhāsissati, ko sundarataraṁ bhāsissati, ko cirataraṁ bhāsissatī’ti.
root_text.sn16.6:6.3: Yato ca kho tumhe, bhikkhave, accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikarotha, taṁ vo mayaṁ paṭiggaṇhāma.
root_text.sn16.6:6.4: Vuddhi hesā, bhikkhave, ariyassa vinaye yo accayaṁ accayato disvā yathādhammaṁ paṭikaroti āyatiñca saṁvaraṁ āpajjatī”ti.

translation_text.sn16.6:1.1: Near Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove.
translation_text.sn16.6:1.2: Then Venerable Mahākassapa went up to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him:
translation_text.sn16.6:1.5: Either you or I should advise the mendicants
translation_text.sn16.6:1.6: and give them a Dhamma talk.”
translation_text.sn16.6:2.1: “Sir, the mendicants these days are hard to admonish, having qualities that make them hard to admonish. They’re impatient, and don’t take instruction respectfully.
translation_text.sn16.6:2.2: Take the monk named Bhaṇḍa, Ānanda’s protégé. He’s been competing in studies with the monk named Abhiñjika, Anuruddha’s protégé. They say:
translation_text.sn16.6:2.3: ‘Come on, monk, who can speak more? Who can speak better? Who can speak longer?’”
translation_text.sn16.6:3.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.sn16.6:3.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell the monk called Bhaṇḍa, Ānanda’s protégé, and the monk called Abhiñjika, Anuruddha’s protégé that
translation_text.sn16.6:3.4: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to those monks and said,
translation_text.sn16.6:4.1: “Yes, reverend,” those monks replied. They went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to them:
translation_text.sn16.6:4.2: “Is it really true, monks, that you’ve been competing in studies, saying:
translation_text.sn16.6:4.3: ‘Come on, monk, who can speak more? Who can speak better? Who can speak longer?’”
translation_text.sn16.6:4.5: “Have you ever known me to teach the Dhamma like this:
translation_text.sn16.6:4.7: see who can recite more and better and longer’?”
translation_text.sn16.6:4.9: “If you’ve never known me to teach the Dhamma like this, what then do you know and see, you futile men, that after going forth in such a well explained teaching and training you’d compete in studies to
translation_text.sn16.6:4.10: see who can speak more and better and longer?”
translation_text.sn16.6:5.1: Then those monks bowed with their heads at the Buddha’s feet and said,
translation_text.sn16.6:5.2: “We made a mistake, sir. It was foolish, stupid, and unskillful of us in that after going forth in such a well explained teaching and training we competed in studies to
translation_text.sn16.6:5.3: see who can speak more and better and longer.
translation_text.sn16.6:5.4: Please, sir, accept our mistake for what it is, so we will restrain ourselves in future.”
translation_text.sn16.6:6.1: “Indeed, monks, you made a mistake. It was foolish, stupid, and unskillful of you to act in that way.
translation_text.sn16.6:6.3: But since you have recognized your mistake for what it is, and have dealt with it properly, I accept it.
translation_text.sn16.6:6.4: For it is growth in the training of the Noble One to recognize a mistake for what it is, deal with it properly, and commit to restraint in the future.”

variant_text.sn16.6:2.2: bhaṇḍañca → bhaṇḍuñca (bj) | abhijikañca → ābhiñjikañca (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed, mr); ābhijjikañca (sya-all, km)
variant_text.sn16.6:5.2: yathābāle yathāmūḷhe yathāakusale → yathā bāle yathā mūḷhe yathā akusale (bj, pts1ed, pts2ed); yathābālaṁ yathāmūḷhaṁ yathāakusalaṁ (?)
variant_text.sn16.6:6.3: taṁ vo mayaṁ → mayaṁ accayaṁ (bj, sya-all)
